input_row.title: Ensure that 'Notify users on password resets?' is set to 'Yes'
input_row.description: Ensure that users are notified on their primary and alternate emails on password resets.
input_row.rationale: User notification on password reset is a proactive way of confirming password reset activity. It helps the user to recognize unauthorized password reset activities. Impact: Users will receive emails alerting them to password changes to both their primary and alternate emails.
input_row.audit: Audit from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select Password reset 5. Under Manage, select Notifications 6. Ensure that Notify users on password resets? is set to Yes
input_row.remediation: Remediate from Azure Portal 1. From Azure Home select the Portal Menu 2. Select Microsoft Entra ID 3. Under Manage, select Users 4. Under Manage, select Password reset 5. Under Manage, select Notifications 6. Set Notify users on password resets? to Yes 7. Click Save Default Value: By default, Notify users on password resets? is set to "Yes". References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/tutorial-enable- sspr#set-up-notifications-and-customizations 2. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity/authentication/concept-sspr- howitworks#notifications 3. https://support.microsoft.com/en-us/account-billing/reset-your-work-or-school- password-using-security-info-23dde81f-08bb-4776-ba72-e6b72b9dda9e 4. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/security/benchmark/azure/mcsb-governance- strategy#gs-6-define-and-implement-identity-and-privileged-access-strategy
input_row.impact: Users will receive emails alerting them to password changes to both their primary and alternate emails.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control can be fully verified via Azure Resource Manager APIs as it relates to configuration settings accessible through the Microsoft Entra ID management APIs.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_entra_id_password_reset_notifications_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:** 
The automated program would check the following:

- **Azure Resource Manager APIs or Azure CLI commands to use:** Use the Microsoft Graph API or Azure CLI to retrieve Password Reset settings from Microsoft Entra ID.
  
- **Specific resources to enumerate:** 
  - Microsoft Entra ID settings related to password reset notifications.
  
- **Exact configuration properties/settings to validate:**
  - Check the `notifyUsersOnPasswordReset` property to determine if it is set to `true`.

- **Clear Pass/Fail criteria with expected values:**
  - **Pass:** If `notifyUsersOnPasswordReset` is `true`
  - **Fail:** If `notifyUsersOnPasswordReset` is `false` or not set.

- **Example API endpoint or CLI command:**
  - Azure CLI example command: 
    ```bash
    az ad password reset notifications show --query "notifyUsersOnPasswordReset"
    ```
  - Microsoft Graph API endpoint (GET request):
    ```
    GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/conditionalAccess/policy
    ```
  - The ``notifyUsersOnPasswordReset`` parameter will need to be checked.

This approach ensures compliance with the control by verifying that users are being notified on password resets, as dictated by the policy established in the benchmarks.
